items.question: Which practice best describes feature creation?
options: Learning vocabulary from text with fit/transform
Hand-designing new columns from domain rules and formulas
Tuning learning rate during model training
Splitting data into train and test folds
items.answer: Hand-designing new columns from domain rules and formulas
items.explanation: Feature creation is explicit column design—ratios, interactions, datetime parts—not learned extraction or training hyperparameters.

items.question: Why add price_per_sqft instead of feeding raw price and sqft to linear regression?
options: Linear models cannot use two numeric columns
The ratio normalizes size so the model compares value per unit area
sqft must always be deleted after division
Ratios remove the need for any scaling
items.answer: The ratio normalizes size so the model compares value per unit area
items.explanation: Derived ratios encode domain structure linear terms may underweight when price and area correlate with home size.

items.question: An interaction feature promo × is_holiday primarily helps which model family?
options: Additive linear models that lack built-in cross-term splits
HashingVectorizer text pipelines only
Unsupervised k-means clustering
Models that ignore numeric inputs
items.answer: Additive linear models that lack built-in cross-term splits
items.explanation: Interactions encode conditional effects; trees can approximate them with splits, but linear models need explicit cross-columns.

items.question: Which datetime encoding treats hour 23 as close to hour 0?
options: Raw integer hour 0–23 only
Sine and cosine pairs of hour
One-hot encoding with 24 columns only
Dropping the hour column entirely
items.answer: Sine and cosine pairs of hour
items.explanation: Cyclical sin/cos maps hours onto a circle so adjacent midnight hours have similar coordinates.

items.question: What is the main leakage risk when aggregating orders per customer?
options: Using left merge instead of inner merge
Including future orders beyond the row's prediction cutoff
Sorting customer_id ascending
Casting counts to float64
items.answer: Including future orders beyond the row's prediction cutoff
items.explanation: Point-in-time aggregates must exclude post-label transactions; otherwise features encode future behavior.
